check_note: Merged site_adjusted site-specific horizon summaries must match the corresponding single-site overall summaries.
Merged site-specific reverse-KM summaries must match the corresponding single-site overall reverse-KM summaries.
Merged site panels must inherit support-tier labels from the corresponding single-site cohort in both horizon summaries and numbers-at-risk tables.
Composition proportions must sum to 1 at every dataset/subgroup/view/time combination.
Risk-set and eligibility counts must decline with horizon; cumulative event/censoring counts must not decrease.
Numbers-at-risk must be monotone non-increasing within each dataset/subgroup series.
Preferred-reporting fields must mark merged overall/sex site_adjusted completeness as primary and site_free as sensitivity only.
Every analysis_structure must include both transition_only and remission_sensitive summaries.
Merged site-contribution totals must reproduce eligible_n in the merged overall/sex completeness summaries.

Merged site-dominance warnings must follow the late-horizon gate: single_site_only always warns; single_site_dominant warns only from year 3 onward and, when configured, outside primary-supported horizons.
Raw group sizes must match Stage 2 n_total fields, time-0 numbers at risk, and reverse-KM record counts.
Numbers-at-risk values at 0,1,...,10 years must be reproducible directly from the raw Stage 2 group data.
Site-level and merged administrative end dates must be reproducible directly from the merged Stage 2 raw data.
